title: Net Profit by Commodity
query: SELECT COALESCE(SUM(TotalSale), 0) - COALESCE((SELECT SUM(TotalCost) FROM MarketBuy WHERE event_timestamp >= :startDate AND event_timestamp < :endDate), 0) AS total FROM MarketSell WHERE event_timestamp >= :startDate AND event_timestamp < :endDate
detailQuery: SELECT COALESCE(s.Type_Localised, s.Type) AS label, (COALESCE(SUM(s.TotalSale), 0) - COALESCE(SUM(b.TotalCost), 0)) AS value FROM MarketSell s LEFT JOIN MarketBuy b ON s.Type = b.Type AND b.event_timestamp >= :startDate AND b.event_timestamp < :endDate WHERE s.event_timestamp >= :startDate AND s.event_timestamp < :endDate GROUP BY s.Type ORDER BY value DESC LIMIT 20

details.text: {total} Cr net profit generated across all commodities during the report period. Not all commodities are created equal — this chart shows where the real money came from and whether your instincts about which goods to haul are validated by the actual margin data.
details.help: Net profit per commodity = sell revenue minus buy cost. total drives the title as the overall net trading result.

tagLines: Commodity net profitability analysis compiled. Margin-by-commodity breakdown noted in the trade record.
Revenue minus cost equals profit. The chart orders them by what you actually kept.
Some commodities look profitable until the fuel, risk, and time cost is factored in. The margins tell the truth.
